brief_notes: Well-structured paper on SE(3)-equivariant ternary complex prediction for targeted protein degradation. Clear architecture description with three novel components (equivariant GNN, ternary inter-graph attention, Pocket Points Decoder). Strong quantitative results showing substantial improvements over AlphaFold3, PRosettaC, and Chai-1. The BSA-DC50 correlation analysis adds practical value. Weaknesses: dataset has a slight inconsistency (4520 total in table vs 4182 after filtering described in text), limited discussion of failure modes, molecular glue performance (DockQ 0.23) is barely acceptable quality, and the paper could better discuss generalization to novel E3 ligases not in training set. Citations are adequate but could include more recent TPD computational work.
